articles.title: Partnerships & Stakeholders: Science and Technology Community
articles.description: The role of global science, academia, research and innovation is enshrined in the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction where it is recognized for the catalytic, boundary-pushing and transformative potential it holds for addressing the dynamic drivers of disaster risk.

articles.title: Leveraging AI to enhance multi-hazard early warning systems
articles.description: This report assesses current applications of AI across all four MHEWS pillars, identifies operational gaps where AI can add value, and outlines the conditions required for responsible and effective implementation of AI.

articles.title: Arab stakeholder groups strengthen partnerships to advance disaster risk reduction across the Arab region
articles.description: Representatives of the Arab stakeholder groups for disaster risk reduction (DRR) convened virtually to strengthen regional collaboration and align priorities with the UNDRR Work Programme 2026–2030.

articles.title: Geneva businesses explore resilience through UNDRR’s ReMA tool in first French-language webinar
articles.description: The United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction (UNDRR), in partnership with the Geneva Chamber of Commerce, Industry and Services (CCIG) and the CCRO Network, successfully delivered the first French-language webinar on the ReMA tool.

articles.title: Launch of the Operational Guidance on Early Warning Systems for Mobile Populations
articles.description: To support the integration of mobility considerations into MHEWS, UNDRR, WMO, ITU, IFRC and IOM jointly developed the Operational Guidance on Early Warning Systems for Mobile Populations. This webinar marks the official launch of the guidance.

articles.title: How cultural spaces offer a cool escape from the heat
articles.description: The heatwaves of today are the coolest heatwaves we are likely to experience for the rest of our lives. SRSG Kamal Kishore reflects on how cultural institutions can play a central role in extreme heat action – and in disaster risk reduction more broadly.

articles.title: UNDRR ONEA & GETI Newsletter 50: Apr-Jun 2026
articles.description: In the second quarter of 2026, UNDRR ONEA-GETI has significantly expanded global disaster risk reduction (DRR) and climate resilience capacities through a series of high-impact training of trainers (ToT) workshops, multi-agency certificate programmes, and localized tools. Key achievements include driving inclusive, whole-of-society resilience approaches by delivering disability-inclusive training in Mongolia, supporting gender-responsive programming for UNICEF India, and engaging over 3,000 global participants through the joint UNDRR-UNOSSC-PAHO-UNTB certificate programme.

articles.title: Morocco advances national early warning systems to protect communities from natural hazards
articles.description: Morocco advances national early warning systems to protect communities from natural hazards

articles.title: Statement on Venezuela earthquakes by Kamal Kishore, Special Representative of the UN Secretary-General for Disaster Risk Reduction
articles.description: Statement on Venezuela earthquakes by Kamal Kishore, Special Representative of the UN Secretary-General for Disaster Risk Reduction
